commit.message:
Feature/dispatch downstream deployments (#4)

* feat: add dispatch-downstream-deployments reusable workflow

Reusable workflow_call workflow that:
- accepts a newline-separated downstream_repos list, an event_type, and
  optional wait_for_downstream / timeout / poll_interval inputs
- builds an org-keyed matrix and mints per-org GitHub App tokens via
  actions/create-github-app-token
- dispatches a repository_dispatch event to each repo with a structured
  client_payload (correlation_id, upstream_repo, run metadata)
- optionally polls each downstream workflow run until completion or timeout
- surfaces a dispatch_result workflow output via a post-matrix summarize job

* docs: update instructions and changelog for pwsh usage and release v1.1.0
* Reorganize README

* Extend CI validation to verify dispatch-downstream-deployments.yml action references

Add PM and QA agents with phased execution

test: causal.js browser-fallback coverage + fix async cleanup race (#35)

* Initial plan

* test: add causal.js coverage and fix browser-fallback async cleanup bug

- Add tests/causal.test.js covering both the Node.js/ALS path and the
  browser stack-fallback path (using vi.resetModules() to force a fresh
  module load without AsyncLocalStorage)
- Add missing tests in tests/chronos.test.js: createChronos throw,
  context-edge creation via contextId, and subgraph() query
- Fix src/causal.js browser-fallback withCause: the try/finally block
  was removing the cause from _stack synchronously even when fn() returned
  a promise, so async callbacks could never read the cause. Guard the sync
  cleanup behind !isAsync so async cleanup is handled solely by
  result.finally()

Coverage impact:
  causal.js  branches  31.81 % → 87.5 %  /  lines  50 % → 100 %
  index.js   branches  83.33 % → 92.59 % /  funcs  81.81 % → 100 %
  Overall    branches  86.76 % → 89.83 % /  lines  95.18 % → 97.9 %

Implements the SSR migration plan from copilot-ssr-plan.md:

## What Changed
- Split monolithic spot-detail-client.tsx into sub-routes for better SEO and performance
- Created dedicated routes: /tips, /prices, /vibes, /gallery under spots/[citySlug]/[spotSlug]/

- layout.tsx fetches core spot data and renders shared header/tabs
- Each tab route (tips/page.tsx, etc.) prefetches its data server-side
- Used HydrationBoundary for seamless client-side hydration
- Default route redirects to /tips tab

commit.message:
perf: batched sensitivity analysis with jax.vmap (#153)

Add vmap-based batched energy evaluation to JAX engines so that
compute_sensitivity() can evaluate all 2N+1 parameter perturbations
in a single vectorized call instead of sequentially.

Engine layer:
- MMEngine base: supports_batched_energy() / batched_energy() with
  sequential fallback for non-JAX backends
- JaxEngine: vmap over _energy_fn with cached JIT compilation
- JaxMDEngine: vmap over _scalar_energy_fn, broadcasting coords
  and neighbor list

Objective layer:
- is_energy_only(): detect pure-energy reference sets
- batched_scores(): dispatch to batched or sequential path
- _batched_energy_scores(): vectorized residual computation

Cycling layer:
- compute_sensitivity() builds (2N+1, P) param matrix upfront
  and calls batched_scores() for the entire sensitivity sweep

Tests:
- 5 new JAX-specific tests covering batched_energy, is_energy_only,
  batched_scores consistency, and end-to-end compute_sensitivity

feat: TurboQuant embedding compression + quick-win optimizations (#277)

* docs(session): wrap cleanup review and merge wave

* feat: TurboQuant embedding compression + quick-win optimizations

Implements TurboQuant-style rotation-preprocessing + scalar quantization
for embedding vector compression (ICLR 2026, arXiv:2504.19874), plus
quick-win infrastructure improvements for the memory subsystem.

TurboQuantCompressor module (engine/src/agent33/memory/quantization.py):
- Random orthogonal rotation (Haar measure via QR, deterministic seed)
- Uniform scalar quantization (configurable 1-8 bits, default 4-bit)
- ~8x compression ratio at 4-bit (3072B float32 -> 395B packed)
- Serialization/deserialization for persistent storage
- Approximate cosine similarity on compressed vectors
- Integrated into EmbeddingCache for transparent compressed caching

Quick-win optimizations:
- Docker: KV cache quantization (q8_0) + flash attention for Ollama
- Config: embedding_dim setting (default 768, matches nomic-embed-text)
- Migration 002: IVFFlat -> HNSW index (m=16, ef_construction=200)
- Vector column: unparameterized Vector() for configurable dimensions

Research deliverables:
- docs/research/turboquant-research.md: algorithm analysis + roadmap
- docs/research/turboquant-security-and-landscape.md: security audit +
  45-paper survey + RTX 3090 feasibility analysis

28 new tests covering round-trip accuracy, similarity preservation,
compression ratios, edge cases, serialization, determinism, and
cache integration.

* fix(ci): fix curl -f flag causing readyz/metrics code concatenation in docker-smoke

The -f (fail) flag makes curl exit non-zero for HTTP >= 400, which
triggers the || echo "000" fallback and concatenates "000" to the
already-written HTTP code from -w (e.g., "503" + "000" = "503000").
Remove -f for checks that expect non-2xx responses, and redirect
stderr to /dev/null instead of stdout.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Add enableSigning config flag for optional signing/verification (#193)

* Add enableSigning config flag to make signing/verification optional (closes #10)

When enableSigning is false (defaults to true for backward compatibility):
- _signAsWriter returns empty string instead of computing signature
- All verification methods return true immediately
- sync() skips signature presence and verification checks
- Topic validators, key update, and load handlers skip signature checks

* Skip topic validator and load signatures when signing disabled

- Skip topic validator registration entirely when enableSigning is false
  to avoid unnecessary per-message decryption overhead
- Skip load request signature computation when signing disabled
- Update JSDoc to document that all signing paths are affected

* Skip raw serialization when signing disabled, add security WARNING

- Conditionally serialize sync messages only when verification is needed
- Add explicit WARNING about security implications of disabling signing
- Document interop incompatibility with signing-enabled peers

* Remove dead enableSigning branch, gate snapshot signing, clarify scope

- Remove unreachable enableSigning check inside already-gated topic validator
- Skip snapshot signature computation when signing is disabled
- Clarify docstring: application-level only, transport signing unaffected

* Skip ACL lookup in load handlers when signing disabled, consistent sync() return

- Short-circuit doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to only fetch writers
  (for requestor identity) when signing disabled, avoiding full ACL lookup
- Return false consistently from sync() on signature rejection (was undefined)

* Use _userPublicKey as requestor when signing disabled, skip ACL lookup

Avoids undefined requestor when writers list is empty during bootstrap,
and eliminates unnecessary ACL call in the signing-disabled path.

* Address review feedback on enableSigning flag

- Add _isSigningEnabled() private helper to centralize config checks
  and reduce drift across call sites
- Refactor doc-load and snapshot-load handlers to use boolean
  `authorized` flag instead of overloading `requestor` variable
- Add empty signature guards in load handlers to cleanly reject
  requests from peers with signing disabled
- Make sync() return type explicit (Promise<boolean>) with
  consistent `return true` on success and `return false` on all
  auth failures
- Replace all inline enableSigning config checks with the helper

* Fix sync() review comments: check load result, guard snapshot signing, document return type

- Check sync() return value in load-response path and return false to try next peer on failure
- Skip snapshot serialization and signature verification when signing is disabled
- Add @since JSDoc annotation documenting the Promise<boolean> return type change

* Address PR #193 review comments: fix JSDoc and add security warnings

- Update sync() JSDoc to acknowledge breaking change from Promise<void>
- Add security warning at snapshot verification bypass when signing disabled
- Expand _sendLoadRequestAndSync() JSDoc to document full return contract

* Clean up topicValidators entry in close() to prevent leaked GossipSub validators

* Update packages/collabswarm/src/collabswarm-document.ts

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix _sendLoadRequestAndSync docstring and add BREAKING CHANGE note to sync()

- Enumerate all false-return cases in _sendLoadRequestAndSync (too-short
  payload, unrecognized keyID) and clarify that only decrypt() failure throws
- Add prominent BREAKING CHANGE callout to sync() JSDoc for the return type
  change from Promise<void> to Promise<boolean>

* Remove incorrect version number from sync() BREAKING CHANGE JSDoc
